name: Batter Bread by James Beard, Chef & Cooking T
recipeInstructions:
1. Proof the yeast in the warm water in a large mixing bowl or the bowl of an electric mixer.
2. Add the dry milk, sugar, butter, salt, optional garlic, and 2 cups flour. Beat by hand or with an electric mixer at medium speed for 2 minutes.
3. Stir in the remaining flour by hand until a soft dough forms. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free place for about 40 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
4. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
5. Uncover the risen dough and stir vigorously for 1 minute.
6. Butter a 1-1/2 quart straight-sided casserole or souffle dish and place the batter inside.
7. Brush the top with egg wash (mix 1 egg white lightly beaten with 1 tablespoon water).
8. Sprinkle sesame seeds over the dough.
9. Bake in the center of the oven for about 1 hour, or until delicately browned and sounds hollow when tapped with a knuckle.
10. Remove from the casserole dish and serve warm, cut into wedges.
